metadata.title: Langevin Autoencoders for Learning Deep Latent Variable Models

sections.title: Motivation
content: Learning deep latent variable models
Latent variable models: p(x; θ) = ∫ p(x | z; θ)p(z)dz
Gradient over model parameters: ∇θ E_p_data(x)[log p(x; θ)] ≈ 1/n Σ ∇θ log p(x_i, z_i; θ)
To learn a latent variable model via gradient ascent, we need to approximate the expectation over the latent posterior p(z | x; θ).
Variational inference is a dominant approach but the approximation power is limited.
Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) is capable of complex posteriors but is slow due to datapoint-wise iterations.
How can we amortize the cost of datapoint-wise iterations?

figures.description: Variational inference posterior density visualization showing Gaussian approximation
Langevin dynamics posterior density visualization showing MCMC sampling

sections.title: Preliminary
content: Langevin dynamics
Langevin dynamics [2] is a gradient-based MCMC.
z' ~ q(z' | z) = N(z'; z + η∇z log p(x, z; θ), 2ηI)
By repeating this iterative update, the samples asymptotically approach the true posterior p(z | x; θ).

sections.title: Method
content: Amortized Langevin dynamics
In our amortized Langevin dynamics (ALD), we prepare an encoder that maps the observed data into the latent variable, and run MCMC on its parameter space.
Markov chain on the latent space is implicitly performed by collecting the output of the encoder.
Our main theorem shows that the ALD is valid as MCMC if the encoder takes the form of: f_z|x(x; Φ) = Φg(x).
Langevin Autoencoder
ALD can be applied to the training of deep latent variable models (DLVMs) by slightly modifying the learning algorithm of traditional autoencoders.
We call this learning algorithm of DLVMs theLangevin autoencoder (LAE).

figures.description: Amortized Langevin dynamics encoder architecture showing the mapping from data to latent space
Update logic diagram showing implicit MCMC over latents

sections.title: Results
content: Toy example
VI fails to capture the multimodality of the true posterior.
Our ALD can approximate complex multimodal posteriors in toy examples.
Image density estimation
Our LAE consistently outperforms VI-based methods such as the VAE and other existing MCMC-based methods.

tables.description: Likelihood evaluation for test data comparing VAE, VAE-flow, Hoffman, and LAE (ours) across MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR-10, and CelebA datasets

sections.title: Future works
content: How can we remove the bias of finite-step MCMC samples?
Is it possible to apply the ALD (and LAE) to the state-of-the-art DLVMs, such as NVAE [5]?

sections.title: References
content: [1] Diederik P Kingma and Max Welling (2013). "Auto-encoding variational bayes." In: arXiv preprint arXiv:1312.6114.[2] Radford M Neal (2011). "MCMC using hamiltonian dynamics." In: Handbook of Markov Chain Monte Carlo.[3] Matthew D Hoffman (2017). "Learning deep latent gaussian models with markov chain monte carlo." In: ICML.[4] Pierre E Jacob et al. (2020). "Unbiased markov chain monte carlo with couplings." In: JRSSB.[5] Arash Vahdat and Jan Kautz (2020). "Nvae: A deep hierarchical variational autoencoder." In: NeurIPS.
